description: A hardy plant that purifies indoor air and thrives in low light.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water sparingly—only when the top inch of soil feels dry. Overwatering can cause root rot.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers indirect sunlight but can tolerate low light conditions, making it ideal for offices or bedrooms.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Grows well in average room temperatures between 18–27°C (65–80°F) and doesn’t require high humidity.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Known for removing toxins like formaldehyde and benzene from the air, improving overall indoor air quality.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed once every 2–3 months during the growing season (spring and summer) with a balanced houseplant fertilizer.

description: Beautiful white blooms that add elegance and clean the air naturally.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Keep the soil consistently moist but not soggy. Peace Lilies droop slightly when they need water—use that as a guide.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers bright, indirect light. Can tolerate low light, but blooming may reduce.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Thrives in temperatures between 18–26°C (65–79°F) with moderate to high humidity levels.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Excellent air purifier that removes toxins such as ammonia and formaldehyde, promoting a healthier indoor environment.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed every 6–8 weeks during spring and summer using a balanced liquid fertilizer diluted to half strength.

description: A soothing plant with medicinal properties and minimal care needs.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Allow the soil to dry out completely between waterings. Aloe Vera stores water in its leaves, so overwatering can easily cause root rot.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers bright, indirect sunlight or partial sun. Too much direct sunlight can cause the leaves to brown or dry out.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Grows best in warm temperatures between 20–30°C (68–86°F) and low to moderate humidity. Keep away from cold drafts or frost.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Known for its healing gel, which soothes burns, cuts, and skin irritations. Also helps purify indoor air.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed sparingly—only once or twice during the growing season (spring and summer) using a diluted succulent fertilizer.

description: Known for its large, fenestrated leaves that bring a tropical vibe.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water when the top 2–3 inches of soil feel dry. Ensure the pot has good drainage to prevent root rot, and reduce watering in winter.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers bright, indirect sunlight. Too little light can slow growth, while direct sunlight may scorch the leaves.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Thrives in warm, humid environments with temperatures between 20–30°C (68–86°F). Mist leaves regularly if indoor air is dry.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Improves indoor air quality and adds a tropical, decorative touch with its iconic split leaves.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed once a month during the growing season (spring and summer) with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer.

description: Tall, glossy leaves that create a bold statement indoors.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Avoid overwatering, as Fiddle Leaf Figs are sensitive to soggy soil and poor drainage.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Needs bright, filtered sunlight to thrive. Too little light causes slow growth, while direct harsh sunlight can scorch the leaves.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Prefers warm temperatures between 21–29°C (70–85°F) and moderate to high humidity. Keep away from cold drafts and sudden temperature changes.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: A stunning decorative plant that enhances indoor air quality and adds a bold, tropical feel to any space.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed every 4–6 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced liquid fertilizer to encourage healthy growth and glossy leaves.

description: Fast-growing vine that tolerates neglect and brightens any space.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water every 1–2 weeks, allowing the soil to dry out between waterings. Pothos is forgiving if you forget occasionally, but avoid letting it sit in water.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Thrives in bright, indirect light but can also tolerate low-light areas. Direct sunlight may scorch the leaves.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Prefers average room temperatures between 18–30°C (65–85°F) and moderate humidity. It adapts well to most indoor conditions.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: A great air purifier that removes common indoor toxins and adds lush greenery to any room. Also easy to propagate from cuttings.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed once a month during the growing season (spring and summer) with a balanced liquid fertilizer diluted to half strength.

description: Low-maintenance plant that produces baby shoots and cleans the air.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Keep the soil lightly moist, but not soggy. Water once the top inch of soil feels dry, and reduce watering in winter.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers bright, indirect sunlight. Can adapt to low light, but too much direct sun may cause leaf tips to brown.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Grows well in average indoor temperatures between 18–27°C (65–80°F) with moderate humidity. Avoid cold drafts.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Excellent air-purifying plant that removes formaldehyde and carbon monoxide. Also safe for pets and easy to propagate.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed every 2–3 weeks during spring and summer with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer for healthy growth.

description: Glossy green leaves and drought tolerance make it perfect for offices.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water only when the soil is completely dry. The ZZ Plant stores water in its thick rhizomes, so it tolerates infrequent watering very well.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Thrives in low to bright, indirect light. Avoid direct sunlight, which can scorch the leaves.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Prefers warm temperatures between 18–26°C (65–80°F) and average indoor humidity. Keep away from cold drafts.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: One of the easiest houseplants to care for — it purifies indoor air and adds a glossy, modern look to any room.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed lightly once every 2–3 months during the growing season with a balanced, diluted fertilizer.

description: Lush green fronds that thrive in humid environments.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Keep the soil consistently moist but not soggy. Water when the top layer of soil begins to dry, and mist regularly to maintain humidity.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Prefers bright, indirect light. Avoid direct sunlight, which can scorch its delicate fronds.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Thrives in temperatures between 18–24°C (65–75°F) and high humidity. Ideal for bathrooms or kitchens where humidity is naturally higher.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: Known for purifying indoor air by removing toxins like formaldehyde and xylene, and adds a lush, green aesthetic to your home.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed every 4–6 weeks during the growing season with a balanced, water-soluble fertilizer to encourage full, healthy fronds.

description: Thick, shiny leaves that make a stylish addition to modern interiors.

details.title: Watering Tips
details.info: Water moderately, allowing the top inch of soil to dry out between waterings. Reduce watering in winter to prevent root rot.

details.title: Light Requirements
details.info: Thrives in bright, indirect light but can tolerate lower light conditions. Avoid direct sunlight, which can burn the leaves.

details.title: Temperature & Humidity
details.info: Prefers temperatures between 18–27°C (65–80°F) and moderate humidity. Keep away from drafts and sudden temperature changes.

details.title: Benefits
details.info: An attractive indoor plant that purifies the air and adds a bold, glossy look to interiors.

details.title: Fertilizing
details.info: Feed every 4–6 weeks during the growing season with a balanced liquid fertilizer to promote healthy, shiny leaves.
